verdict.answer_first: A leads closer to the bottom line: "two that matter most are the missing error handling and the lack of any authorization"; B starts broader with "has a few real problems" before ranking.
verdict.decisiveness: B is more decisive on ship/no-ship: "auth check (#1) and the field allowlist (#2) are the ones I'd treat as blocking"; A ends with a softer conditional: "For a throwaway internal endpoint..."
verdict.fabrication: B over-asserts more: "likely password hash, email, tokens" from an unknown user object; A phrases the same risk conditionally: "If that row has..."
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives one tightened route with validation, authz, 404, allowlist, and catch in place; cite: "A tightened version:"
